prompt: Month-end close keeps paging the on-call for a non-problem and I want it dead this quarter. Our expense rollup prints one line per cost center plus a TOTAL line, and every NUMBER on it is correct — I have reconciled it against the ledger three times. But the row order comes out different on every single run. Same ledger entries, run the report twice, two differently-ordered reports; the reconciliation tooling diffs tonight's report against last night's, sees all 26 cost centers 'move', and pages somebody at 2am. The runbook contract (and the test fixtures) say rows come out in cost-center code order, A to Z, TOTAL last, every run, byte-identical for identical ledgers. Repro is easy: `cargo test --offline` — the arithmetic assertions always pass, the ordering assertions flap from run to run. Fix src/lib.rs so the report is deterministic and in the contracted order; don't touch tests/, Cargo.toml, or .gitignore.
